messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Building Autonomous AI Agents
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Building Autonomous AI Agents
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Enhance your computational workflows by integrating advanced automation into your daily scientific research practices. Building Autonomous AI Agents for Scientific Research is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 26?27, led by Renan Maestri (Department of Ecology, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul). By mastering autonomous AI agents, environmental researchers can automate complex data retrieval and code generation tasks directly within their R-based ecological workflows. This training enables you to build secure, local or cloud-based agentic systems that scale your data-intensive analyses without compromising methodological transparency. You will learn to leverage these advanced tools to accelerate literature synthesis and streamline the processing of large-scale environmental datasets.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/building-autonomous-ai-agents-for-scient) to reserve your place, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last chance: Online course: AI Agents for Researchers
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Online course: AI Agents for Researchers
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Last chance - Are you looking to streamline your complex data workflows and improve your research efficiency? AI Agents for Researchers is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 5 with Martin Qiu (Lazaridis School of Business and Economics, Wilfrid Laurier University). This upcoming seminar introduces agentic AI workflows that can help you automate complex ecological data preparation and streamline environmental modeling pipelines. By learning to structure AI agents for file-based, auditable tasks, you can ensure your R-based analyses and simulation workflows remain fully reproducible and transparent. These advanced techniques will enable you to manage large datasets and document your research code with greater efficiency and rigor.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/ai-agents-for-researchers) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online live course: Evolutionary macroecology in R: exploring biodiversity patterns at large scales
messages.subject_clean: Online live course: Evolutionary macroecology in R: exploring biodiversity patterns at large scales
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

There are two spots available for the course "Evolutionary macroecology 
in R: exploring biodiversity patterns at large scales".

Dates & schedule: June 8th-12th, 2026 from 13:00-17:30 (Madrid time 
zone).

Course webpage: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/ecology/macroecology-in-r-exploring-biodiversity-patterns-at-large-scales/

The aim of this course is to introduce participants to macroecological 
analyses using R to explore large-scale biodiversity patterns and 
ecological processes.

By the end of the course, students will be able to:

  	* Generate geographic maps of biodiversity.
  	* Construct biogeographic regions while accounting for the 
evolutionary relationship of species.
  	* Integrate tools of macroevolutionary dynamics, such as estimating 
ancestral areas and ancestral traits, with community phylogenetic 
metrics.

Instruction will combine lectures, hands-on computational exercises, and 
guided examples. Language of instruction: English.

For any questions, please write to courses at transmittingscience.com

Best wishes

Sole

-- 
Soledad De Esteban-Trivigno, PhD
Director
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com/courses

Bluesky @soledeesteban.bsky.social
X @SoleDeEsteban
Orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2049-0890

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Reproducible Data Analysis with R (Online Course, 20–23 July)
messages.subject_clean: Reproducible Data Analysis with R (Online Course, 20–23 July)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce the new dates for the course Reproducible Data Analysis with R, which will take place online from 20?23 July.
 
Reproducibility is a cornerstone of reliable and transparent science, yet many computational workflows remain difficult to reproduce in practice. This course is designed to address these challenges directly by teaching practical, modern tools and workflows for building fully reproducible data analyses in R.
Through a combination of lectures, live coding, and hands-on exercises, participants will learn how to structure, document, and share their analyses in a way that is transparent, robust, and easy to maintain over time.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-reproducibility/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/r-reproducibility/ ) 
 
Key topics include:
Building well-structured R projects and research compendia
Creating dynamic reports with Quarto
Version control and collaboration with Git and GitHub
Managing dependencies reproducibly with renv
Sharing data using DOI-linked repositories
Using Docker to fully encapsulate computational environments
By the end of the course, participants will be able to design complete reproducible workflows?from project setup to containerised analyses?ready for collaboration and long-term reuse.
 
The course is aimed at researchers, data scientists, and R users who want to improve the reliability and transparency of their computational work. Basic experience with R is recommended (e.g., importing data and creating simple plots or tables).
 
 Schedule (Berlin time, 9:00?12:00 daily):
20 July: Introduction to reproducibility, RStudio projects, Quarto
21 July: Git & GitHub, collaboration workflows, research compendia
22 July: Dependency management with renv, data sharing and DOIs
23 July: Docker, containerisation, and integrating full workflows
 
 
Best regards, Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Single Cell Data Analysis
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Single Cell Data Analysis
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Analyzing complex biological systems at a granular scale is increasingly vital for modern environmental and ecological research. Single Cell Data Analysis 2.0 led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This on-demand seminar provides comprehensive training in preprocessing, clustering, and integrating high-dimensional single-cell datasets using R and Python. Ecologists and environmental scientists can apply these advanced analytical workflows to resolve cellular heterogeneity in complex organisms, tracking physiological responses to environmental stressors at unprecedented resolution. By mastering these computational pipelines, you can integrate diverse genomic datasets to uncover the molecular mechanisms driving ecological adaptation and evolutionary resilience.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/single-cell-data-analysis-20-1) at your own pace and please share this with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Phylogenetic Comparative Methods in R – June 15–19 (6th Edition)
messages.subject_clean: Phylogenetic Comparative Methods in R – June 15–19 (6th Edition)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Are you interested in Phylogenetic Comparative Methods in R?
We are pleased to announce the 6th edition of this online Physalia course, which will run from June 15th to 19th.
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course44/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course44/ )
 
This course provides a hands-on introduction to how phylogenetic comparative methods can be used to study trait evolution and diversification using time-calibrated phylogenies.
 
Over five days, participants will combine lectures and practical sessions to learn how to:
Build and interpret ultrametric phylogenies (chronograms)
Reconstruct trait evolution across lineages
Explore models of diversification and evolutionary rates
Apply phylogenetic comparative methods in R
The course is aimed at advanced students, researchers, and professionals with a basic background in evolutionary biology and some familiarity with R. Participants are also welcome to bring their own datasets for discussion and practical guidance.
 
 
 
Best regards, Carlo




--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last chance: Online course: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Online course: Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Last chance - Enhance your quantitative research toolkit with advanced modeling techniques designed for complex, real-world data. Nonlinear Structural Equation Modeling is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 11 with Holmes Finch (Department of Educational Psychology - Ball State University). Ecologists and environmental scientists can use these advanced statistical methods to model complex, non-linear relationships in ecosystems, such as soil-vegetation dynamics and hydrological feedback loops. By mastering these techniques in R and Mplus, you will be able to specify latent interactions and curvilinear growth trajectories that traditional linear models fail to capture. This hands-on training provides the reproducible code and diagnostic tools needed to directly enhance your upcoming publications and grant proposals.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/nonlinear-structural-equation-modeling) to reserve your place before it starts, and feel free to circulate this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Last chance: Online course: Practical Meta-Analysis for Beginners
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Online course: Practical Meta-Analysis for Beginners
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Last chance to join our 2-day seminar Practical Meta-Analysis for Beginners livestreaming June 22 and 24 with Centre for Applied Statistics Courses (CASC) (University College London). This upcoming livestream seminar provides a comprehensive introduction to conducting and interpreting meta-analyses, with an optional hands-on session in R. Environmental scientists and ecologists can apply these systematic methods to synthesize conflicting findings across diverse studies on vegetation dynamics, soil chemistry, or hydrological patterns. By learning to model heterogeneity and address publication bias, you will be able to derive robust, field-wide conclusions that significantly advance your ecological research.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/practical-meta-analysis-for-beginners-1) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Live online course: Multivariate trait evolution in R
messages.subject_clean: Live online course: Multivariate trait evolution in R
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

Transmitting Science is offering a new edition of the course "Modelling 
and analysing multivariate traits evolution using R".

Course webpage: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/evolution/modelling-and-analysing-multivariate-traits-evolution-using-r/

The aim of this course is to introduce participants to methods for 
modelling and analysing the evolution of multivariate traits using the 
mvMORPH R package.

By the end of the course, students will be able to:

  	* Understand multivariate trait evolution models.
  	* Apply comparative methods using R.
  	* Interpret evolutionary patterns in multivariate datasets.

The course comprises lectures, hands-on computational exercises and 
guided examples.

For any questions, please write to courses at transmittingscience.com

Best regards,

Haris

-- 
Haris Saslis, PhD
Course Coordinator
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com [1]



Links:
------
[1] http://www.transmittingscience.com

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Longitudinal Analysis in Biostatistics - Advanced Longitudinal Modeling in R
messages.subject_clean: Longitudinal Analysis in Biostatistics - Advanced Longitudinal Modeling in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Enhancing your statistical toolkit is essential for analyzing complex environmental datasets over time. Longitudinal Analysis in Biostatistics led by Md Tuhin Sheikh (Biostatistics, College of Health Solutions, Arizona State University) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. Ecologists and environmental scientists frequently collect repeated measurements over time to track changes in vegetation, soils, and hydrology. By mastering advanced longitudinal modeling frameworks, you can effectively account for temporal correlation and missing data in your environmental datasets. This on-demand seminar provides the practical R-based skills needed to resolve these complex hierarchical structures and robustly analyze your ecological observations.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/longitudinal-analysis-in-biostatistics) on demand at your own pace, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

Last chance - Enhance your computational workflows by learning to deploy autonomous AI tools directly within R. Building Autonomous AI Agents for Scientific Research is a 2-day seminar livestreaming June 26?27 with Renan Maestri (Department of Ecology, Universidade Federal do Rio Grande do Sul). This upcoming seminar demonstrates how to construct autonomous AI agents that can plan tasks, use tools, and retrieve information directly within R-based workflows. By integrating these agentic systems into your computational research, you can automate complex data-gathering, code generation, and literature synthesis for ecological and environmental analyses. This approach allows you to scale your research productivity and manage data-intensive modeling without sacrificing methodological rigor or reproducibility.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/building-autonomous-ai-agents-for-scient) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Gaussian Processes with PyMC
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Gaussian Processes with PyMC
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Enhance your environmental data analysis with powerful new Bayesian modeling techniques. Gaussian Processes with PyMC and LLMs led by Chris Fonnesbeck (PyMC Labs and Vanderbilt University Medical Center) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. Ecologists and environmental scientists can apply these advanced Gaussian process models to capture complex spatial and temporal patterns in vegetation, soil, and hydrological datasets. By integrating PyMC with LLM-assisted workflows, you can efficiently write and scale robust Bayesian models that account for non-linear trends and key sources of uncertainty. This on-demand training provides the practical coding skills needed to translate messy field observations into publication-ready ecological insights.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/gaussian-processes-with-pymc-and-llms-1) online at your convenience and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online short course: Handling environmental variables with R
messages.subject_clean: Online short course: Handling environmental variables with R
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

Registration is open for the Transmitting Science course "Environmental 
variables: how to download and process them with R"

Course webpage:

https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/ecology/environmental-variables-how-to-obtain-and-process-them-with-r/ 
[1]

Course overview:

Georeferenced maps of environmental variables are currently essential 
for a range of ecological and biogeographical analyses, from species 
distribution and ecological niche models to global biodiversity trends. 
Such maps are now widely available in multiple online sources, although 
they often need to be processed to become compatible with each other and 
with the remaining data in the analysis (e.g. species occurrence or 
abundance).

In this short course, we will use a range of tools to:

1) Import maps of climatic, topographic and anthropogenic variables from 
various sources into R (e.g. WorldClim, ENVIREM, CHELSA, AquaMaps, 
MARSPEC, Bio-ORACLE, SEDAC, CORINE)

2) Obtain the values of these variables for point locations (e.g. 
species occurrence sites, random background samples) and for polygons 
(e.g. grid cells, provinces and other spatial divisions

3) Try out different ways of converting categorical variables to 
continuous form, including proportions and distances

4) Adjust the spatial extent and resolution of different maps, so that 
they appropriately match each other as well as additional data in the 
analysis.

Best regards,

Haris

-- 
Haris Saslis, PhD
Course Coordinator
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com [2]



Links:
------
[1] 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/ecology/environmental-variables-how-to-obtain-and-process-them-with-r/
[2] http://www.transmittingscience.com

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-ECOLOGY members

Enhance your ecological research by mastering advanced statistical workflows for complex environmental datasets. Multivariate Analysis of Biotic Communities led by Oliver Miler (Northwest Indian Fisheries Commission) is now available as an on-demand streaming seminar. This on-demand seminar provides researchers with the practical skills needed to conduct advanced multivariate analysis of biotic communities using R. By mastering these analytical workflows, you can better understand complex relationships between biological communities and their environmental drivers in your own ecological research. The curriculum covers essential data preprocessing, visualization, and multivariate modeling techniques to help you extract meaningful insights from large environmental datasets.

Access this seminar (https://instats.org/seminar/multivariate-analysis-of-biotic-communit-1) online at your convenience and we'd appreciate you sharing this with colleagues and students!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
